reasoning: The informal statement (Rudin Ex 2.27b) is standard and unambiguous: condensation points are points where every neighborhood contains uncountably many points of E. The corrected version correctly formalizes this as `P = {x | ∀ U ∈ 𝓝 x, ¬ Set.Countable (U ∩ E)}`. The original formalization has a critical error: `P = {x | ∀ U ∈ 𝓝 x, (P ∩ E).Nonempty ∧ ¬ Set.Countable (P ∩ E)}` uses `P ∩ E` instead of `U ∩ E`, making the condition (a) self-referential on P and (b) independent of the neighborhood U. This produces an incomparable statement that is actually FALSE: take k=1, E=Set.univ, P=∅; then P∩E=∅ is not Nonempty, so the RHS of hP becomes ∅ (since 𝓝 x is nonempty, ∀U, False is False), making hP hold vacuously, while E\P=univ is uncountable, contradicting the conclusion. The error is a misformalization of the condensation point condition — the formalizer misread how to express "every neighborhood contains uncountably many points of E".
